+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

McLEAN, Va. - April 28, 2026 - Three attorneys from global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP were selected for inclusion on the 2026 Virginia Super Lawyers "Rising Stars" list, recognizing outstanding young lawyers across a range of practice areas.

Super Lawyers evaluates attorneys statewide across more than 70 practice areas. Candidates are identified through a rigorous selection process that combines peer nominations with independent research and evaluation.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG TOKYO HOSTS ANREV BRIEFING ON REAL ASSETS, DATA CENTRES AND AI

TOKYO - April 30, 2026 - Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Tokyo office recently hosted and sponsored a briefing for the Asian Association for Investors in Non‑Listed Real Estate Vehicles (ANREV), bringing together institutional investors and industry professionals to discuss emerging trends in real assets, data centres, and artificial intelligence.

The program featured a panel discussion titled "Real Assets & New Technologies: Data Centres & AI Opportunities," with Greenberg Traurig Shareholder Joel H. Rothstein, chair of the firm's Asia Real Estate Practice, among the speakers. Panelists discussed how advances in AI are reshaping capital allocation, infrastructure planning, and investment strategies across global real asset markets, with a particular focus on the growing demand for data centre assets throughout the Asia‑Pacific region.

+ GOODWIN PROCTER LLP FLOW NEUROSCIENCE ANNOUNCES FDA APPROVAL OF WORLDS FIRST AT-HOME BRAIN STIMULATION TREATMENT FOR DEPRESSION

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP AXELERA AI SECURES OVER $250M IN RECORD EU AI SEMICONDUCTOR INVESTMENT

Axelera AI, an Edge AI hardware startup, has raised more than $250 million in its most recent funding round.

Orrick advised Axelera AI on the transaction.

THE COMPANY Axelera AI is a European semiconductor leader developing advanced AI acceleration hardware for edge computing and data centre applications, driving the next generation of artificial intelligence across Europe.

Source : Company Website

+ AGATHA KLUK JOINS ORRICKS NEW YORK TECH COMPANIES GROUP

Leading tech companies advisor Agatha Kluk joins Orrick as a partner in New York. Agatha is a sought-after counselor to founders, operators, board members and venture investors in the NY tech ecosystem. She advises clients throughout the lifecycle - from formation to venture financing to exit - in the consumer tech, AI, health and wellness, fintech and cleantech sectors. Agatha joins from Perkins Coie, which in 2022 combined with Kluk Farber Law, the 12-lawyer venture law boutique she founded. Agatha began her legal career at Debevoise & Plimpton.

Source : Company Website

+ ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLPREALTEK SECURES KEY ANTITRUST WIN IN SMART TV CHIP DISPUTE

Semiconductor company Realtek Semiconductor Corp. secured a key win in its dispute over a "bounty" patent licensing agreement by competitors that included a $1 million incentive to sue Realtek with the District Court of the Northern District of California denying the defendants' early motion for summary judgment. American Lawyer recognized the win in its Litigators of the Week column.

Source : Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JH9-V3J3-RWJ3-Y215-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - INDUSTRY IN FOCUS: TRENDS & PEER ACTIVITY2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - INDUSTRY IN FOCUS: TRENDS & PEER ACTIVITY

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ PILLSBURY OPENS IN BOSTON WITH A FOCUS ON TECHNOLOGY, LIFE SCIENCES AND FINANCIAL INDUSTRIES

Pillsbury is opening a new office in Boston, marking an important expansion into one of the nation's leading growth and innovation centers. The office will launch with a team of five senior attorneys and additional associates, who bring diverse transactional and litigation backgrounds.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURYS MARQUEE INSURANCE RECOVERY & ADVISORY PRACTICE CONTINUES EXPANSION, WELCOMING RENOWNED POLICYHOLDER LITIGATOR BOB GILBERT

Robert J. Gilbert, a nationally recognized litigator who represents policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters, has joined Pillsbury from Latham & Watkins. He comes to the firm as one of the founding partners of Pillsbury's newly established Boston office.

Source: Company Website

+ AS NOVEL DIGITAL RIGHTS ISSUES COME TO THE FORE, PILLSBURY ADDS IP LITIGATOR ROBERT L. URIARTE IN LOS ANGELES

Pillsbury has further enhanced its expansive tech-focused capabilities with the addition of Intellectual Property litigator and trial lawyer Robert L. Uriarte as a partner in its Los Angeles office.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE RANKS 44TH ON FORTUNE'S "100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR®" LIST FOR 2026

SEATTLE (April 1, 2026)-Perkins Coie is proud to announce that it has again been named to Fortune's "100 Best Companies to Work For®" list for 2026, marking the 24th consecutive year that the firm has been featured on Fortune's prestigious list.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. + REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. + REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

+ Wynn Resorts, Limited (WYNN: $96.21) Market Cap $10 billion, Gaming sector, P/E: 30.4, PV$1000 (20 yrs): $2,447

Wynn Resorts, Limited (NASDAQ:WYNN $96.21), the NASDAQ's largest Gaming company by market cap, has sold off $11.36 (or 10.6%) in the past week to close at $96.21.

Activities

Wynn Resorts, Limited is a premier global developer and operator of luxury integrated resorts, primarily focused on high-end gaming, hospitality, and entertainment experiences. Headquartered in Las Vegas, the company owns and manages iconic properties such as Wynn Las Vegas, Encore Las Vegas, Wynn Macau, and Encore Boston Harbor, offering premium accommodations, casinos, fine dining, retail, and live events. Its core business revolves around creating upscale destinations that blend gaming with world-class amenities, catering to affluent clientele in key markets like the United States and Asia. The fastest-growing segments include international operations, particularly in Macau, where demand for integrated resorts has surged due to expanding tourism and gaming preferences in the region. Additionally, Wynn is advancing digital initiatives and non-gaming revenue streams, such as enhanced entertainment and wellness offerings, to diversify and sustain growth amid evolving consumer trends. This strategic focus positions the company as a leader in the luxury hospitality sector, driving long-term value through innovation and operational excellence. It is the NASDAQ's largest Gaming company by market capitalisation.

Wynn Resorts, Limited (NASDAQ:WYNN) is a preeminent player in the global luxury hospitality sector, renowned for its integrated resort management, particularly in Las Vegas and Macau. The company's strategic commitment to sustainability is underscored in the Wynn Resorts 2025 sustainability report, which highlights significant strides in environmental and social governance. Wynn Macau's achievements are notable, with projects like renewable energy and water conservation in 2025 standing out. Their carbon reduction and waste initiatives provide competitive advantages, aligning with growing consumer demand for responsible travel. Initiatives such as the Wynn Macau LEED certification and energy efficiency upgrades further solidify its market position. The Wynn Resorts ESG initiatives 2025 emphasize a robust corporate sustainability strategy fostering community engagement and maintaining a focus on responsible gaming programs. As investors seek companies with strong ESG performance, Wynn's proactive climate risk disclosures and biodiversity initiatives contribute to an impressive WYNN ESG score, enhancing its long-term growth potential.

Short Stock Dashboard

+ Dollar Tree, Inc. (DLTR: $86.80) Market Cap $17 billion, Retailing sector, P/E: 13.9, PV$1000 (20 yrs): $9,713

Dollar Tree, Inc. (NASDAQ:DLTR $86.80), the NASDAQ's 6th largest Retailer company by market cap, has sold off $9.71 (or 10.1%) in the past week to close at $86.80.

Activities

Dollar Tree, Inc., a prominent retailer listed on the Nasdaq, operates a vast network of discount stores across the United States and Canada, primarily under the Dollar Tree and Family Dollar brands. The company's core business involves sourcing and selling a wide array of everyday essentials, including household goods, consumables, seasonal items, and party supplies at fixed low prices, catering to value-conscious consumers. This model emphasizes efficient supply chain management and private-label products to maintain competitive pricing. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have included e-commerce initiatives, which have expanded online sales through enhanced digital platforms, and the health and beauty category, driven by rising demand for affordable personal care items. Additionally, multi-price point offerings in Family Dollar stores have fueled growth by attracting a broader customer base amid economic uncertainties, supporting overall revenue expansion. It is the NASDAQ's 6th largest Retailer by market capitalisation.

Dollar Tree, Inc. (NASDAQ:DLTR) operates with a distinct value retail business model, positioning itself strongly within the low-cost retail sector. The company strategically capitalizes on its vast product range, including Dollar Tree party supplies, household essentials, and seasonal decorations, to draw cost-conscious consumers. Known for Dollar Tree home decor and cleaning supplies, it has built a significant market presence, appealing to those seeking affordable options for Dollar Tree snacks, beverages, and candy. Beyond consumables, Dollar Tree crafts a formidable niche with Dollar Tree craft supplies and school supplies, emphasizing its commitment to providing comprehensive solutions. Its competitive edge is further enhanced by innovations in Dollar Tree kitchenware and Dollar Tree beauty products. Dollar Tree's growth outlook is promising, with potential expansion through Dollar Tree bulk packs and value packs, and customer loyalty bolstered by Dollar Tree clearance deals, ensuring continued strategic relevance in offering Dollar Tree seasonal Halloween decorations and more.

Emerson Electric Co. (NYSE:EMR $137.76), the NYSE's 3rd largest Industrial company by market cap, has pulled back $10.23 (or 6.9%) in the past week to close at $137.76. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 79.1 points (or 1.1%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -8.0%.

Activities

Emerson Electric Co., a diversified global technology and engineering firm founded in 1890 and headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri, specializes in automation, climate technologies, and tools for industrial, commercial, and residential markets. The company's core business activities include delivering process management solutions, industrial automation systems, and network power technologies that enhance efficiency across sectors like manufacturing, energy, and healthcare. Emerson's fastest-growing segments are its automation solutions and digital transformation offerings, fueled by rising demand for Internet of Things (IoT) integration and artificial intelligence in smart manufacturing. This strategic focus on innovation and sustainability has solidified Emerson's position as a leading provider of reliable, energy-efficient technologies, supporting global infrastructure and operational advancements. It is the NYSE's 3rd largest Industrial company by market capitalisation.

Emerson Electric Co. (NYSE:EMR) is a global leader in Emerson OT cybersecurity patch management and industrial automation solutions. The OPSWAT Emerson partnership patch management enhances their Emerson patch management for industrial control systems, providing robust Emerson OT patching solutions. Emerson cybersecurity solutions for OT, including DeltaV cybersecurity and PlantWeb asset management cybersecurity, strengthen their market position. Emerson OT vulnerability management is critical for Industrial control system patch management Emerson, supported by Emerson remote patch management tools like AMS Device Manager Emerson. The innovative PlantWeb digital ecosystem is integral to Emerson process automation solutions alongside their Fisher control valve, Rosemount pressure transmitter, and Micro Motion Coriolis flow meter offerings. Emerson measurement instrumentation, including Emerson control valves, Emerson flow meters, and Emerson pressure transmitters, underscore their product portfolio. With Ovation control system security, Emerson safety instrumented systems (SIS), and Emerson industrial cybersecurity services, they offer comprehensive Emerson secure device management. Emerson fieldbus device security and Emerson industrial automation cybersecurity ensure Emerson asset optimization solutions. Emerson diagnostics predictive maintenance, Emerson remote monitoring and management, and Emerson condition monitoring support sustained operational efficiency.

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT $121.48), the NYSE's 2nd largest Broadline retailer company by market cap, has pulled back $8.71 (or 6.7%) in the past week to close at $121.48. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 79.1 points (or 1.1%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -7.8%.

Activities

Target Corporation, listed on the New York Stock Exchange (USNYSE), is a leading American retail company that operates a nationwide network of discount department stores and an integrated e-commerce platform. Its core business activities center on offering a diverse range of general merchandise, including apparel, home furnishings, electronics, groceries, and beauty products, with a strong emphasis on affordable pricing, private-label brands, and convenient shopping experiences. The company's fastest-growing segments include digital and omnichannel retail, such as online sales, curbside pickup, and same-day delivery services, which have expanded rapidly amid shifting consumer preferences for seamless shopping. Additionally, Target's health and wellness categories, along with its owned brands, have seen significant growth due to increased demand for value-driven, essential goods. This strategic focus on innovation and customer-centric services solidifies Target's position as a major player in the competitive retail sector. It is the NYSE's largest Broadline retailer by market capitalisation.

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) is a leading American retail company offering a diverse range of products through Target online shopping and Target.com. The company enhances customer convenience with services like Target same-day delivery, Target Drive Up, Target curbside pickup, and Shipt delivery. Shoppers can also utilize features such as Target order pickup and the Target app for a seamless experience. Target online grocery and Target grocery delivery are complemented by Good & Gather groceries. Customers benefit from Target Circle deals, the Target RedCard, and insights from the Target weekly ad to find Target clearance deals. Product offerings include Target electronics, such as Target TVs, Target laptops, and Target tablets, alongside Target home decor, Hearth & Hand with Magnolia, Threshold home, Target furniture, Target bedding, and Target mattresses. The store also features Target beauty products and Target skincare, with fashion lines like Cat & Jack kids clothing. Essentials include Up & Up household essentials, Target baby products, and Target diapers.

KeyCorp (NYSE:KEY $20.75), the NYSE's 17th largest Bank by market cap, has pulled back $1.48 (or 6.7%) in the past week to close at $20.75. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 79.1 points (or 1.1%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -7.7%.

Activities

KeyCorp is a prominent U.S.-based financial services holding company, headquartered in Cleveland, Ohio, and traded on the New York Stock Exchange. As a leading bank holding company, it operates primarily through its subsidiary, KeyBank, delivering a broad array of services including retail banking, commercial lending, investment management, and consumer finance to individuals, businesses, and institutions. The company's core activities emphasize providing accessible financial solutions, such as deposit accounts, loans, mortgages, and advisory services, while prioritizing customer-centric innovation and risk management. KeyCorp's fastest-growing segments include digital banking, which has expanded rapidly through mobile and online platforms to meet rising demand for convenient services, as well as commercial banking and wealth management, fueled by economic growth and increased client needs for tailored investment and lending products. This strategic focus has enabled KeyCorp to strengthen its market presence and adapt to evolving financial landscapes. It is the NYSE's 13th largest Bank by market capitalisation.

KeyCorp (NYSE:KEY) is a leading financial institution with a diversified business model spanning KeyBank personal loans, KeyBank savings account, KeyBank checking account, and KeyBank mortgage rates. Strategic involvement from activist investor Cane Capital, evidenced by their 13D filing and Cane Capital KeyCorp investment, signals potential shareholder activism aimed at enhancing KeyCorp shareholder value. Recently, Cane Capital buys KeyCorp stake, sparking significant KeyCorp investor news. The company's comprehensive services, including KeyBank wealth management, Key Private Bank services, and Key corporate banking, position it well in the competitive banking sector. KeyCorp stock garners investor interest through its robust KEY dividend and strategic KEY stock buyback programs. As KeyCorp earnings report continues to attract attention, investors remain focused on KeyCorp proxy fight developments. Seamless integration of Key mobile banking app and Key online banking enhances customer experience, reaffirming KeyCorp's commitment to innovation and growth.

Past Week: International Flavors & Fragrances Inc. (NYSE:IFF) pulls back 7% on firm volume

International Flavors & Fragrances Inc. (NYSE:IFF $77.48), the NYSE's 8th largest Chemicals company by market cap, has pulled back $5.45 (or 6.6%) in the past week to close at $77.48. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 79.1 points (or 1.1%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -7.6%.

Activities

International Flavors & Fragrances Inc. (IFF), a leading global company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in the creation, development, and supply of flavors, fragrances, and other specialty ingredients for diverse industries including food and beverage, personal care, household products, and pharmaceuticals. As a key innovator in sensory experiences, IFF leverages advanced technology and sustainable practices to deliver high-quality solutions that enhance product appeal and functionality. The company's core business revolves around its three main segments: Nourish, which focuses on flavors and ingredients for the food and nutrition sector; Scent, encompassing fragrances for consumer goods; and Health & Biosciences, which produces enzymes, cultures, and probiotics. Among these, the Health & Biosciences segment has emerged as the fastest-growing area, driven by rising demand for natural, health-oriented ingredients amid global trends in wellness and sustainability, positioning IFF for continued expansion in emerging markets. It is the NYSE's 8th largest Chemicals company by market capitalisation.

International Flavors & Fragrances Inc. (NYSE:IFF) is a leading entity in the flavors and fragrances sector, offering a diversified portfolio of natural extracts, essential oils, and fine fragrance ingredients. Renowned for its innovation in beverage flavors and dairy flavors, the company delivers cutting-edge flavor systems, encapsulated flavors, and advanced taste modulators. Its strategic use of encapsulation technologies and emulsifiers and stabilizers positions it at the forefront of texture and mouthfeel solutions, enhancing the sensory experience in food and beverages. IFF’s commitment to sustainability is evident in its use of fermentation-derived ingredients and plant-based proteins. The firm leverages probiotics and cultures, along with micronutrient premixes, to cater to health-conscious consumers. Its incorporation of fragrance ingredients into home fragrance solutions and personal care products demonstrates its versatility. With a robust footprint in international markets, IFF holds strong growth potential driven by continuous product innovation and strategic partnerships.

Past Week: The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) slides 6% on robust volume

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD $302.55), the NYSE's largest Retailer company by market cap, has slid $20.50 (or 6.3%) in the past week to close at $302.55. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 79.1 points (or 1.1%) in the week, this represented a relative price change of -7.4%.

Activities

The Home Depot, Inc., a leading home improvement retailer listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in selling a wide range of products and services for DIY enthusiasts and professional contractors. Founded in 1978 and headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, the company operates over 2,300 stores across the United States, Canada, and Mexico, offering tools, building materials, appliances, and home decor through both physical and online channels. Its core business revolves around providing one-stop solutions for home improvement projects, including installation services and product expertise. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have included e-commerce, which has expanded rapidly with digital tools and same-day delivery options, and the professional customer segment, catering to contractors and tradespeople with specialized procurement and supply chain services. This growth is driven by increasing demand for home renovations and online shopping, positioning The Home Depot as a resilient player in the retail sector. It is the NYSE's largest Retailer by market capitalisation.

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) exemplifies a robust business model, capitalizing on diverse product offerings such as Home Depot power tools, Home Depot kitchen appliances, and Home Depot smart home devices. As a leading retailer, Home Depot captures substantial market share through strategic positioning in sectors like Home Depot lumber and building materials, leveraging significant competitive strengths in Home Depot paint, flooring, and lighting fixtures. urn:contentItem:6JK3-D173-RX2K-114G-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:14ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN EXPANDS TO BOSTON WITH NEW OFFICE LAUNCH

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman is set to open an office in Boston, expanding its footprint in one of the largest legal and business markets in the United States. On 12th May 2026, the firm announced that it will commence operations with a team of five senior lawyers, including a notable litigator hired from Latham & Watkins.Robert Gilbert, who joined Pillsbury after a seven-year tenure at Latham, specializes in representing policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters. He has previously acted for prominent Boston-area clients such as Harvard University, UniFirst Corporation, and Northland Investment Corporation. Gilbert will be instrumental in launching the Boston office alongside key figures from Pillsbury's leadership, including Michael Zeliger, Andrew Troop, Don Lonczak, and David Simons.David Dekker, chair and CEO of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman, emphasized the strategic importance of the Boston office, citing the city's dynamic start-up ecosystem, world-class research institutions, and a concentration of financial services companies and private capital investors. He remarked, "Pillsbury has long represented clients headquartered in the Boston area and those connected to the Boston-Cambridge innovation ecosystem. Opening a Boston office is a natural next step given our capabilities and relationships in the tech, life sciences, healthcare, and financial industries in Boston and around the world."This new office marks Pillsbury’s 17th location in the U.S. and its 25th globally. The firm joins a growing list of major law firms that have established a presence in Boston, aiming to leverage the city's thriving technology and life sciences sectors, as well as its expanding financial services market. Recent entrants include Reed Smith, which opened in March, along with a slew of other firms like Freshfields and Simpson Thacher & Bartlett.With its Boston office, Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman is poised to deepen its engagement with local clients and industries, further solidifying its role in the evolving legal landscape of the region.

+ MORGAN LEWIS STRENGTHENS EMPLOYEE BENEFITS TEAM WITH PILLSBURY TALENT

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP has announced the addition of a prominent attorney from Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP to its Employee Benefits team. This strategic move is seen as a response to the growing complexities in employee benefits law and the increasing demand for expert legal guidance in this area. The attorney, whose experience spans multiple facets of employee benefits, is expected to enhance Morgan Lewis’ capabilities in providing comprehensive legal services to its clients.The announcement comes as Morgan Lewis looks to bolster its offerings in employee benefits law, particularly in the wake of several recent regulatory changes. The attorney's arrival on 08th April 2026 is intended to strengthen the firm's position in an increasingly competitive legal landscape. Morgan Lewis has consistently focused on expanding its practice areas to meet client needs, and this latest addition is part of that ongoing strategy.Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman, known for its robust practice in employee benefits, has seen a number of its attorneys transition to other firms in recent years. The departing attorney brings a wealth of experience in compliance and regulatory issues, which will be invaluable to Morgan Lewis as it navigates the complexities of employee benefit plans and pension laws. This move not only reflects the attorney's individual career advancement but also highlights the fluid nature of talent within the legal sector.Morgan Lewis has a reputation for its deep expertise in various practice areas, and the inclusion of the Pillsbury attorney is expected to further enhance its service offerings. The firm has been proactive in adapting to changes in legislation affecting employee benefits, ensuring that its clients remain compliant and well-informed. By integrating new talent, Morgan Lewis aims to provide even more specialized services tailored to the evolving needs of its diverse client base.As the legal field continues to evolve, the strategic hiring of top talent from firms like Pillsbury is critical for maintaining a competitive edge. Morgan Lewis’ latest hiring signals its commitment to delivering high-quality legal solutions in employee benefits, reinforcing its reputation as a leading firm in this practice area. The legal community will be watching closely to see how this new addition impacts Morgan Lewis’ capabilities and client service in the months ahead.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 News That Matters]

+ ASHURST PERKINS COIE MERGER GAINS STRONG SUPPORT FROM PARTNERS

The merger of global law firms Ashurst and Perkins Coie into the entity Ashurst Perkins Coie has received overwhelming support from the partnerships of both firms. This decision was finalized following a consultation period, with the approval announced on 13th April 2026. The merger is expected to close in the third quarter of the year, contingent upon customary closing conditions.Mark Birnbaum, executive committee chair of Perkins Coie, emphasized the significance of this approval, stating that it reflects the partners' commitment to creating a forward-looking firm. He noted that the merger underlines a shared confidence in their capacity to grow and deliver exceptional legal services in an evolving global market. Paul Jenkins, CEO of Ashurst, also highlighted the alignment between the two firms, asserting that their combined strengths will benefit their clients significantly.The merged firm aims to focus on key sectors including technology, energy, infrastructure, and financial services. Bill Malley, firmwide managing partner at Perkins Coie, expressed optimism that the combination would enhance the firms' abilities to meet clients’ complex cross-border legal needs. The new entity is set to boast approximately 3,000 lawyers across 52 offices in 32 countries, with major hubs located in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York.The merger, first announced last year, brings together two firms with combined revenues of around USD 2.8 billion. Their lawyers have extensive experience in international markets, having worked in cities such as Brussels, Dubai, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Paris, Seoul, Shenzhen, Singapore, and Tokyo. The leadership of the new firm will be shared by Malley and Jenkins as global co-CEOs, while Ashurst's global chair, Karen Davies, and Perkins Coie's corporate practice chair, Brian Eiting, will serve as co-chairs.As the legal landscape continues to evolve, the Ashurst Perkins Coie combination is poised to enhance its competitive edge by leveraging the strengths of both firms. This strategic move not only positions the new entity to better serve its clients but also sets the stage for sustained growth and performance in the legal industry.

+ PERKINS COIE AND ASHURST UNITE TO FORM ASHURST PERKINS COIE

NEW YORK — Perkins Coie and Ashurst announced today that the partnerships of both firms have voted overwhelmingly to approve their proposed combination. The approval follows consultation from both firms and reflects strong support for the combination of two law firms with highly complementary geographic reach, sector depth, and practice strength. The date of this announcement is recorded as 2026-04-14 16:18:02.950901.Upon closing, Ashurst Perkins Coie will emerge as a globally integrated firm with combined revenues of approximately US$2.8 billion. The new entity will boast around 3,000 lawyers across more than 50 offices, including flagship hubs in Seattle, London, Sydney, and New York. Ashurst Perkins Coie will be particularly noted for its strengths in the technology, energy and infrastructure, and financial services sectors—industries that are pivotal to the ongoing global economic transformation.Bill Malley, the Firmwide Managing Partner at Perkins Coie, remarked on the significance of this merger by stating, 'Our partnership's approval reflects a shared belief that combining to form Ashurst Perkins Coie will create a truly differentiated global legal platform—one with the scale, sector depth, and technological leadership to meet our clients' increasingly complex, cross-border needs.' This sentiment underscores the strategic vision behind the combination.Paul Jenkins, Global CEO of Ashurst, echoed Malley’s sentiments, emphasizing the strong alignment between the two firms. 'This vote confirms the strong alignment between our firms and our joint ambition for the future,' he stated, highlighting the complementary expertise across sectors and practice areas that will benefit clients. Jenkins added that the merger will create a compelling platform for top legal talent.The combination remains subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to finalize in the third quarter of 2026. As the legal landscape becomes more complex, Ashurst Perkins Coie aims to position itself as a trusted advisor, ready to navigate the intricacies of the global market and deliver exceptional legal services.

+ PERKINS COIE REENTERS CHINA MARKET WITH NEW SHENZHEN OFFICE

Perkins Coie has officially opened a representative office in Shenzhen, marking the firm's significant comeback in the Chinese market. This development comes on the heels of a strategic decision to re-establish an on-the-ground presence in the region, with the office inaugurated on 14th April 2026.The Seattle-based law firm had previously operated offices in Beijing and Shanghai, which were closed in 2024. At that time, Perkins Coie indicated plans to pivot towards Shenzhen, citing the city’s rapid growth as a key tech and manufacturing hub. The firm aims to leverage this new office to enhance its support for Asia-based companies navigating US and cross-border legal needs.Leading the Shenzhen office will be Bing Ai, serving as chief representative and managing partner, along with Wei Yuan, who will act as a representative and litigation partner. Both lawyers are US-trained and bring extensive experience in advising technology-focused clients. Ai specializes in patent procurement and portfolio management in sectors such as semiconductors and medical devices, while Yuan is adept at handling patent infringement disputes.Bill Malley, Perkins Coie’s managing partner, emphasized the firm's commitment to providing US legal expertise across borders. He noted that the opening of the Shenzhen office reflects a dedication to assisting clients in navigating the complexities of global markets, especially in light of the increasing challenges they face.As Perkins Coie re-establishes its presence in mainland China, it stands apart from the trend of office closures and headcount reductions by many leading US firms due to geopolitical tensions and regulatory challenges. However, several international firms have recently opened offices in Shenzhen, attracted by its status within the Greater Bay Area, which has seen substantial economic growth. Perkins Coie’s strategic move comes as the firm prepares for a potential merger with UK-based Ashurst, which would create a top 20 global firm with projected annual revenues of around $2.7 billion.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [2 News That Matters]

+ CROSBY SECURES $60M IN FUNDING, BACKED BY COOLEY LLP

Contract review start-up Crosby has successfully raised $60 million in its latest funding round, marking its third capital raise in less than a year. The funding announcement was made on 3rd April 2026, highlighting the firm’s rapid growth and the interest from prominent investors, including the West Coast-based law firm Cooley LLP.The Series B round was co-led by Lux Capital and Index Ventures, with participation from renowned venture capital firms such as Sequoia, 01 Advisors, and Bain Capital Ventures, as well as angel investor Elad Gil. With this new influx of capital, Crosby plans to enhance its technology capabilities and provide better support to its growing customer base, which includes notable software development firms Clay and Rogo, as well as the financial services operations platform Ramp.Crosby combines artificial intelligence with legal expertise to expedite the contract review process. The firm reported a significant increase in the value of its negotiated contracts, which has soared to just over $1 billion. This is a remarkable rise from the $30 million in brokered contracts when Crosby emerged from stealth mode last year, showcasing the firm’s impressive trajectory in the legal tech space.In a recent blog post, Crosby emphasized its commitment to innovation, stating, 'As we’ve scaled, we’ve also started experimenting with entirely new technology experiences.' The firm has introduced various features, including simulations of counterparty responses to contract redlines, voice agents that negotiate on behalf of clients, and a platform that allows clients to oversee and provide input on Crosby’s work.Founded in 2024 by Ryan Daniels and John Sarihan, Crosby has quickly established itself in the legal tech landscape. Daniels, a former associate at Cooley, has an extensive background in tech entrepreneurship, while Sarihan brings experience as a former Google software engineer and tech lead for Crosby client Ramp. Together, they are steering Crosby towards a future of enhanced contract negotiation and review, supported by substantial investments from Cooley and other leading firms.

+ COOLEY LLP ENHANCES TECH PRACTICE WITH FRESHFIELDS ALUMNI

Cooley LLP has announced a significant expansion of its technology practice, bringing on board several attorneys who previously worked at Freshfields Bruckhaus Deringer. This strategic move, aimed at bolstering Cooley's capabilities in the tech sector, highlights the firm's commitment to remaining competitive in an increasingly dynamic legal landscape.As of 23rd November 2025, Cooley's latest recruitment drive underscores the firm's ongoing efforts to enhance its service offerings in technology, a sector that continues to evolve rapidly. The addition of these high-profile attorneys from Freshfields is expected to not only strengthen Cooley's existing practice but also attract new clients seeking expert legal guidance in technology-related matters.The new team members bring a wealth of experience, having advised prominent clients on a range of legal issues, including intellectual property, data protection, and corporate transactions in the tech industry. Their expertise is anticipated to complement Cooley’s established reputation in venture capital and startup advisory services, further positioning the firm as a leading player in the technology legal sector.Cooley LLP’s decision to recruit from Freshfields reflects a broader trend within the legal industry, where firms are actively seeking to acquire top talent to respond to the demands of a fast-paced, technology-driven market. As legal challenges in the tech sector become more complex, the need for specialized knowledge and skills is paramount, making these strategic hires particularly timely.With this expansion, Cooley LLP aims to solidify its standing as a go-to destination for clients in the technology field, reinforcing its capabilities in an area that is critical for innovation and growth. As the firm integrates its new attorneys, stakeholders will be watching closely to see how these changes impact Cooley's market position and client services in the coming months.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ REED SMITH FACILITATES £11M ACQUISITION IN SOFTWARE SECTOR

ReedSmith LLP has played a pivotal role in the successful £11 million takeover of a software business by its founder. The acquisition, which has garnered attention in the technology investment community, showcases Reed Smith's expertise in corporate law transactions. On the 8th of May 2026, the legal firm announced the completion of the deal, marking a significant milestone for both the founder and the software company involved.The founder's acquisition is expected to not only enhance the operational capabilities of the software business but also provide new opportunities for innovation within the sector. Reed Smith's involvement in the transaction underscores its commitment to supporting entrepreneurial ventures in navigating the complexities of corporate mergers and acquisitions. The firm’s team provided comprehensive legal advice throughout the process, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements and facilitating negotiations between the parties.As technology continues to advance rapidly, the software industry remains a focal point for investment and growth. Analysts suggest that Reed Smith's guidance in this takeover aligns with broader trends indicating a surge in strategic acquisitions aimed at bolstering competitive advantage within the market. The legal firm's proficiency in managing such transactions positions it favorably among peers in the legal industry.This acquisition could potentially reshape the landscape for the software business, as the founder plans to implement strategic changes aimed at expanding its market reach. With Reed Smith’s backing, the founder is anticipated to leverage the company's existing assets while integrating new technologies to enhance service delivery. Industry experts are watching closely to see how these developments unfold in the coming months.Reed Smith's successful navigation of this £11 million takeover reflects its strong reputation in providing tailored legal solutions to clients in the technology sector. The firm’s strategic advisory role is expected to resonate well with investors, as the software business embarks on a new chapter under the founder's leadership, backed by the robust legal framework provided by Reed Smith.

+ REED SMITH EXPANDS REAL ESTATE TEAM WITH STINSON'S FINANCE ATTORNEY

ReedSmith LLP has announced the addition of a seasoned real estate finance attorney from Stinson LLP, marking a strategic move to bolster its Washington, D.C. office. This development aligns with Reed Smith's ongoing efforts to enhance its capabilities in the competitive real estate sector. The attorney's extensive experience in real estate finance will be instrumental in addressing the evolving needs of clients seeking legal expertise in this area.The new hire arrives at Reed Smith amidst a vibrant real estate market, where demand for legal services has surged. As of 7th May 2026, the firm is poised to leverage this attorney's background to deliver innovative solutions and comprehensive legal support in complex transactions. This strategic hire underscores Reed Smith's commitment to providing top-notch legal services, particularly in the realms of real estate and finance.Reed Smith's D.C. office has been a focal point for its real estate practice, and the inclusion of this attorney is expected to strengthen the firm's position in the market. The attorney’s prior work at Stinson LLP involved significant projects that encompassed financing, acquisition, and development, which will complement Reed Smith's existing offerings. This move is anticipated to enhance the firm's client roster and project portfolio.The legal landscape for real estate finance continues to evolve, with increased regulatory scrutiny and changing market dynamics. By adding expertise from Stinson, Reed Smith aims to navigate these complexities more effectively. The firm is committed to anticipating client needs and adapting to market trends, making this acquisition a timely and strategic enhancement.Reed Smith's ongoing expansion efforts highlight its ambition to remain a leader in the real estate legal sector. The firm is dedicated to attracting top talent that can contribute to its growth and innovation in legal services. This latest addition from Stinson LLP demonstrates Reed Smith's proactive approach in strengthening its practice and enhancing the value it provides to clients in an ever-changing environment.

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS PRIVATE EQUITY PRACTICE WITH NEW PARTNER IN LA

ReedSmith LLP has announced the addition of a new private equity partner to its Los Angeles office, further solidifying its position in the competitive legal landscape. This strategic move is expected to enhance the firm's offerings in private equity, particularly in a region that has become increasingly vital for investment activities. The firm has been actively expanding its capabilities to address the growing demand from clients in this sector.The newly appointed partner, who joins Reed Smith on 4th May 2026, brings a wealth of experience in private equity transactions and related advisory services. This addition is part of Reed Smith's broader strategy to strengthen its private equity practice and provide comprehensive legal services to its clients, including investment firms and corporate entities involved in mergers and acquisitions.Reed Smith's Los Angeles office is poised to benefit significantly from this new partnership, as the region continues to attract substantial capital inflows into private equity. The firm aims to leverage the partner's expertise to navigate complex transactions and provide tailored solutions that meet the evolving needs of its clients in this fast-paced market. This hire aligns with Reed Smith's commitment to delivering high-quality legal services and fostering long-term relationships with its clients.As the private equity landscape evolves, firms like Reed Smith are recognizing the importance of having experienced professionals who can guide clients through intricate investment structures and regulatory frameworks. The addition of a seasoned partner enhances Reed Smith's ability to offer strategic insights and innovative solutions, reinforcing its reputation as a leading player in the legal sector.With this strategic hire, Reed Smith LLP is well-positioned to capitalize on growth opportunities within the private equity domain. The firm’s commitment to excellence and its proactive approach to meeting client demands will likely contribute to its continued success in a competitive marketplace.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI P.C. [3 News That Matters]

+ WILSON SONSINI ADVISES BIOORBIT ON $13.2 MILLION SEED FUNDING

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. has successfully advised BioOrbit, a pioneering space manufacturing company, on its recent $13.2 million (£9.8 million) seed funding round. This funding round, announced on 30th April 2026, was co-led by prominent investors LocalGlobe and BREEGA, along with contributions from Seedcamp, Auxxo Female Catalyst Fund, Type One Ventures, and 7percent Ventures, among others.BioOrbit, led by Cambridge scientist Dr. Katie King and oncology researcher Dr. Leonor Teles, aims to establish a pharmaceuticals factory in space within the next decade. The company is focused on developing hardware that enables the large-scale crystallization of protein drugs, a critical process for the production of biopharmaceuticals. The successful fundraising effort illustrates the growing interest in innovative solutions within the life sciences sector, particularly in the context of space applications.The Wilson Sonsini team that provided legal counsel to BioOrbit during this financing included Daniel Glazer, Jose Campos, Rashad Mohammed, and Sophia Kunthara. Their expertise in corporate governance and emerging company strategies has positioned BioOrbit to further advance its ambitious plans for space-based pharmaceutical manufacturing.As the life sciences landscape continues to evolve, the intersection of technology and pharmaceuticals is becoming increasingly significant. BioOrbit's unique proposition of leveraging space for drug manufacturing represents a potential paradigm shift in how biopharmaceuticals are produced. The involvement of Wilson Sonsini in this transaction underscores its commitment to supporting innovative companies that are pushing the boundaries of traditional industries.For more insights into the funding and BioOrbit's future plans, interested parties can view the company's latest updates on LinkedIn or refer to additional coverage available on UKTN. The developments in this sector are being closely monitored by investors as they consider the implications of space-based manufacturing on the pharmaceutical industry.

+ WILSON SONSINI ENRICHES ENERGY AND CLIMATE SOLUTIONS PRACTICE WITH KEN SANOCKI

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati, a leading provider of legal services to technology and life sciences sectors, has announced the addition of Ken Sanocki as a partner in its Energy and Climate Solutions (ECS) practice. His appointment aims to bolster the firm's capacity to guide clients through the intricate landscape of energy infrastructure financing, which is vital to the ongoing energy transition. This strategic move comes on the heels of the firm's commitment to expanding its capabilities in this rapidly evolving sector.Sanocki's previous role as vice president of legal at Sunrun, the largest residential solar and storage provider in the United States, positions him uniquely to contribute to Wilson Sonsini. Since 5th May 2026, he has been instrumental in overseeing all legal aspects of project finance, including tax equity financings and complex debt structures that support large-scale distributed energy projects. His experience includes leading more than 50 tax equity financings exceeding $10 billion across various distributed energy portfolios, underscoring his deep expertise in the field.Doug Clark, managing partner of Wilson Sonsini, expressed enthusiasm about Sanocki's arrival, stating, "Ken has operated at the center of the energy transition, helping shape the financing structures that have enabled distributed energy to scale into mainstream infrastructure." His extensive knowledge of tax equity and structured financings, combined with his commercial instincts, will provide clients with valuable guidance as they navigate the complexities of energy financing.At Wilson Sonsini, Sanocki will focus on advising companies, sponsors, and investors in structured and project finance across renewable energy, storage, and climate infrastructure. His role will also encompass supporting clients in capital formation and strategic transactions, including the development of revenue-generating asset portfolios and assisting firms preparing for IPOs or M&A activities. The firm is committed to enhancing its advisory services in clean energy and infrastructure, evident from its recent partner additions who specialize in structured finance and regulatory matters.Sanocki's background includes serving as senior counsel for global structured and project finance at SunEdison and beginning his career as a project finance associate at Milbank. He holds a J.D. from New York University School of Law and a B.A. from Boston College. His joining, alongside recent partner hires, positions Wilson Sonsini as a formidable player in sophisticated energy transition finance, reinforcing its market leadership in clean energy advisory.

+ WILSON SONSINI GUIDES OFF PISTE CAPITAL IN FUND LAUNCH

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati P.C. has announced its advisory role in the launch of Off Piste Capital's inaugural fund. These appointments represent the eighth, ninth, and tenth partner additions in Morrison Foerster’s London office over the past year, reflecting the firm's commitment to meeting the growing client demand across AI, fintech, data centers, technology infrastructure, and digital transformation.Eric McCrath, Chair of Morrison Foerster, remarked, 'Technology is at the heart of everything we do at Morrison Foerster. + MORRISON & FOERSTER ANALYZES SENATE'S BAN ON PREDICTION MARKET TRADING

In a significant move for the financial markets, the Senate has unanimously approved a resolution banning Senators and their staff from participating in prediction markets. This decision was made on 29th April 2026, reflecting growing concerns about the integrity of financial and political processes. Morrison & Foerster observes that this initiative is part of a broader effort to regulate prediction markets, which have garnered both interest and skepticism in recent years.Hours after the Senate resolution, Senators Kristen Gillibrand (D-NY) and Dave McCormick (R-PA) introduced legislation aimed at preventing government officials from trading on prediction markets. The proposed bill seeks to establish standards on insider trading, implement anti-money laundering measures, and create an independent Office of the Retail Advocate within the CFTC. Morrison & Foerster notes that these changes are likely to reshape the landscape for prediction markets, emphasizing consumer protection.In a related development, Kalshi, a regulated exchange for prediction markets, announced new features designed to restrict underage access to its platform. The measures include mandatory face ID for users and two-factor authentication for parents. Kalshi has stated that these updates align with the legislative framework proposed by Senators Gillibrand and McCormick, highlighting the proactive approach the company is taking in response to regulatory changes. Morrison & Foerster suggests that such measures could foster greater trust in prediction markets among consumers.The Senate Commerce, Science, and Transportation Committee is set to hold a hearing on 20th May 2026, addressing issues surrounding prediction markets and sports betting. Former Representative Patrick McHenry will testify alongside industry representatives, making this an important event for stakeholders in both sectors. Morrison & Foerster emphasizes that the outcomes of this hearing could influence future regulations affecting prediction markets.Additionally, CFTC Chairman Michael Selig has defended the commission's exclusive jurisdiction over prediction markets, countering claims of lax regulation. In a letter to The Wall Street Journal, Selig described prediction markets as innovative financial products rather than mere gambling sites. Morrison & Foerster highlights that this defense is crucial for maintaining the legitimacy of prediction markets amid increasing scrutiny from lawmakers and the public.

S O PAULO, May 8, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP represented Enter, a Brazil-based artificial intelligence legal technology company, in its recent Series B financing round. Its AI agents independently handle activities ranging from evidence discovery to drafting responses and negotiating settlements, all of which are subsequently reviewed by legal professionals within Enter's network.The Greenberg Traurig deal team that advised Enter on this transaction was led by Matthew Squires, a shareholder in the Corporate and Latin America practice based in Salt Lake City. Frank Cordero from the Miami office, Agata Jacqueline Paramesvari, a New York Corporate associate, and Nathalya Zara, a Corporate Law Clerk/J.D. from Fort Lauderdale.With approximately 3,100 lawyers across 51 locations globally, Greenberg Traurig is well-positioned to provide innovative legal services that transcend borders and industries. + GREENBERG TRAURIG STRENGTHENS CHICAGO PRACTICE WITH ADDITION OF GERARD C. MARTIN

Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP has expanded its Chicago office by adding seasoned secured finance attorney Gerard C. Martin as a shareholder in the firm's Restructuring & Special Situations Practice. His expertise encompasses cash flow and asset-based loans, leveraged loans, acquisition financings, and complex intercreditor arrangements. Additionally, Martin maintains an active practice in equipment and aviation finance, where he represents various parties in aircraft and engine financings and project finance matters.A significant aspect of Martin's practice involves financial restructurings, where he advises secured creditors and debtors in both in-court and out-of-court transactions. + GREENBERG TRAURIG FACILITATES MAJOR HYPERSCALE DATA CENTER SALE IN JAPAN

Global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP represented the seller in the disposition of a stabilized hyperscale data center located in Keihanna, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan, near Osaka. This transaction, completed on 7th May 2026, is among the largest asset-level sales of a fully leased, operational hyperscale data center in the Japanese market to date.The approximately 40-megawatt facility is leased to a single U.S. hyperscale cloud provider. The seller is a joint venture between a fund sponsored by a U.S. institutional manager and a fund sponsored by a Japanese trading company. The buyer is a consortium that includes Singapore-listed CapitaLand Ascendas REIT, which acquired a 49% interest, and a fund managed by an affiliate of the Japanese trading company, which acquired the remaining 51%.This transaction marks the second Japanese hyperscale data center sale on which Greenberg Traurig has advised this client, following a previous transaction completed in November. The firm's expertise in digital infrastructure has positioned it as a leader in this rapidly evolving sector.Leading the deal team representing the seller at Greenberg Traurig were Max Sternberg, of counsel in the firm's London and Chicago offices, and Ayako Kawano, a shareholder in Tokyo. The team also included Anja Giddings, a Berlin Real Estate Associate, and Judicial Scrivener Takayo Himeji, along with support from Tokyo Real Estate Local Partner Hiroshi Hara, Tokyo Corporate Associate Sayaka Ishimoto, and Washington, D.C. Technology, Media & Telecommunications Associate Trina Kwon.Greenberg Traurig's Digital Infrastructure Industry Group comprises over 110 attorneys worldwide, offering a comprehensive range of legal services across various practice areas. The firm’s global presence and multidisciplinary approach enable it to effectively advise clients in the data center, digital infrastructure, and telecommunications industries on a wide array of transactions, from hyperscale leasing to joint ventures.
